---
title: "Final Project"
subtitle: "An original piece of research conducted in R "
---

Check back soon for details of your final project

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Key Dates

| Component        | Due Date        |
|-------------------|-----------------|
| Research Proposal | Friday, July 10 |
| Initial Analysis  | Monday, July 20 |
| Final report      | Friday, July 24 |
| Presentations     | Friday, July 24 |


<!--

All written submissions are due by **11:59pm** and submitted through google docs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Project Requirements

### Dataset

-   At least **100 observations** and **5 variables**
-   Must be real data (not simulated)
-   Suggested sources: [TidyTuesday](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday), [Kaggle](https://www.kaggle.com), [UCI ML Repository](https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/index.php), government open data portals
-   You must have a **research question** your analysis addresses

### Report (rendered as HTML from `.qmd`)

Your Quarto report must include all of the following sections:

1.  **Introduction** — Background, research question, and why it matters.
2.  **Data** — Source, collection method, variables of interest, and any cleaning steps.
3.  **Exploratory Analysis** — At least 3 visualizations with written interpretation.
4.  **Inferential Analysis** — At least one formal statistical test or model (confidence interval, hypothesis test, or regression).
5.  **Conclusion** — Summary of findings, limitations, and potential next steps.
6.  **References** — Dataset citation and any other sources.

All code must appear in the rendered document (`echo: true`).

### Presentation (10 minutes)

-   Slides built in Quarto (`format: revealjs` recommended)
-   Cover your question, key findings, and one visualization
-   7 minutes presenting + 3 minutes Q&A

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Grading Rubric

| Component                                                | Points  |
|----------------------------------------------------------|---------|
| Proposal (clear question + viable dataset)               | 10      |
| Exploratory analysis (3+ visualizations, interpretation) | 20      |
| Inferential analysis (correct method, interpretation)    | 25      |
| Writing quality (clarity, structure, flow)               | 15      |
| Reproducibility (renders without errors, code shown)     | 15      |
| Presentation (clarity, visuals, Q&A)                     | 15      |
| **Total**                                                | **100** |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Report Template

Start from this template to ensure your report has the required structure.

```` markdown
---
title: "Your Project Title"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
    echo: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

[Describe your research question and why it is interesting.]

## Data

```{{r}}
library(tidyverse)
data <- read_csv("your_data.csv")
glimpse(data)
```

[Describe the dataset: source, variables, observations.]

## Exploratory Analysis

```{{r}}
# Visualization 1
```

[Interpret the plot.]

## Inferential Analysis

```{{r}}
# Your statistical test or model
```

[Interpret results in context.]

## Conclusion

[Summarize findings, discuss limitations, suggest future work.]

## References

- Dataset: [Source name and URL]
````

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Proposal Guidelines

Your one-page proposal should answer:

1.  **What is your research question?** Be specific (e.g., "Is there a relationship between sleep hours and GPA among college students?" rather than "I want to study sleep").
2.  **What dataset will you use?** Provide the source URL and describe the key variables.
3.  **What statistical methods do you plan to use?** Reference specific methods from class.

Submit as a PDF on Canvas by **March 28 at 11:59pm**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: callout-important
## Academic Integrity

All analysis must be your own work. Properly cite your data source. If you use code from external sources (Stack Overflow, documentation, etc.), include a comment with the URL. Passing off others' analysis or AI-generated code as your own is a violation of the academic integrity policy.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Past Project Examples

Below are a few examples of strong projects from previous semesters (shared with permission):

-   *Predicting Avocado Prices with Linear Regression* — used USDA retail price data
-   *Racial Disparities in Traffic Stops: A Hypothesis Testing Approach* — used Stanford Open Policing Project data
-   *Does Weather Affect Bike-Share Ridership?* — used Chicago Divvy trip data with NOAA weather data

-->
